file_name: HEMISPHERX - Sales, Marketing, Distribution, and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Product" means an injectable formulation of clinical grade Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived).
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'an injectable formulation of clinical grade' from the definition of 'Product' introduces ambiguity. Later sections, specifically Exhibit 1, refer to product specifications and Certificate of Analysis, implying a certain grade and formulation. By removing the initial specification, the later requirements for analytical specification become vague and unenforceable, as there is no baseline grade or type of Interferon alfa-n3 to compare against. This omission creates an in-text contradiction by referencing quality standards for a product whose essential characteristics have been undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 1 The drug, Interferon Alfa-n3, is intended for investigational use in the countries in which it is distributed prior to receipt of RAA; The drug, Interferon Alfa-n3, meets your specifications as reflected on the attached Certificate of Analysis; The drug, Interferon Alfa-n3, is not in conflict with the laws of the countries in which it is distributed; The investigation will be conducted in accordance with good clinical practices, including review and approval of the study by an independent ethics panel and informed consent of the study subjects; The drug, Interferon Alfa-n3, does not present an imminent hazard to public health, either in the United States, if the drug were to be reimported, or in the countries in which it is distributed; The drug, Interferon Alfa-n3, is labelled in accordance with the laws of the countries in which it is distributed. I have reviewed the attached labels and the current Certificate of Analysis against the specifications and agree with the above statements that these meet the laws of the countries in which the product will be distributed. Signature: __________________________________ Date: ______________________ Printed Name: Saleh Al- Rashid Title: Chairman and CEO Company: Scientific Products Pharmaceutical Co. LTD
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'injectable formulation of clinical grade' specification from the definition of "Product." Exhibit 1 then refers to the product meeting certain specifications reflected in a Certificate of Analysis. By removing the requirement for a specific grade or formulation, the changed definition creates a contradiction. The Certificate of Analysis and product specifications now lack a clear baseline definition for the 'Product' they are analyzing, rendering the quality control requirements vague and potentially unenforceable. This is a clear and direct conflict, as the later sections expect a defined product with specific characteristics, while the revised definition removes these characteristics.

perturbation.original_text: SCIEN will have six 6) months after the date of this Agreement to Purchase at least 50 vials to be used by the MOH in treating patients with MERS. Scien will thereafter, based on the outcome of the initial treatment for MERS by the MOH trial, aggressively promote to all stakeholders in Saudi Arabia and the other GCC states("First Performance Milestone").
perturbation.changed_text: SCIEN will aggressively promote to all stakeholders in Saudi Arabia and the other GCC states.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the initial requirement for SCIEN to purchase at least 50 vials for treating MERS patients and defining the former action as "First Performance Milestone" creates an in-text contradiction. Section VI.B.1. states Termination for breach will include Failure to purchase Product and distribute to End Users as called for in II D. By removing the 50 vials requirement, there is a contradition on what performance milestones should be looked at. This introduces a contradiction as what constitutes breach is ambigous, since the milestone has been remove and is not clear what will be evaluated for the term agreement to be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination for breach will include: 1. Failure to purchase Product and distribute to End Users as called for in II D. 2. Failure of SCIEN achieving less than 50% achievement of the minimum Purchases as in III B.7. for two (2) consecutive years, 3. Insolvency, or the filing for protection under either Party's bankruptcy laws. Upon the filing of a petition in bankruptcy, insolvency or reorganization against or by either Party, or either Party becoming subject to a composition for creditors , whether by law or agreement, or either party going into receivership or otherwise becoming insolvent (such party hereinafter referred to as the "insolvent party"), this Agreement may be terminated by the other Party by giving written notice of termination to the insolvent Party, such termination immediately effective upon the giving of such notice of termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the initial requirement for SCIEN to purchase at least 50 vials for treating MERS patients, which was previously defined as a "First Performance Milestone". This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that "Termination for breach will include: 1. Failure to purchase Product and distribute to End Users as called for in II D.". By removing the 50 vials requirement, the condition for termination due to failure to purchase and distribute the product becomes ambiguous, creating a clear conflict and potential unenforceability. The contradiction is not minor and would cause confusion as to what obligations SCIEN is required to perform to avoid termination, since the milestone has been remove and is not clear what will be evaluated for the term agreement to be terminated.

perturbation.original_text: The audits shall be conducted upon reasonable advance notice during regular business hours at HEMISPHERX's principal office and in such a manner as not to unduly interfere with HEMISPHERX's operations.
perturbation.changed_text: The audits shall be conducted in such a manner as not to unduly interfere with HEMISPHERX's operations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detail of 'reasonable advance notice during regular business hours at HEMISPHERX's principal office' from the audit clause, creates an in-text contradiction as the location and time, is not specified. Section 5 of Exhibit 4 refers to audits needing to be requested at least twenty (20) business days in advance, this detail is now removed, and the timeline is no longer specified, and location becomes unresovable, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HEMISPHERX may perform audits for initial qualification of SCIEN as well as periodic audits and "for cause" audits. At mutually agreed upon times, HEMISPHERX may review standard operating and other quality control procedures and records and the records of SCIEN relating to the Agreement. Such routine and general oversight review is to be requested at least twenty (20) business days in advance, limited to two (2) persons, completed within one (1) to two (2) business days and shall be offered to HEMISPHERX one (1) time each calendar year. SCIEN will make every reasonable effort to accommodate the special circumstances that may arise pursuant to "for cause" audits. The following applies to all audits: · Prior to an audit HEMISPHERX will communicate to SCIEN the scope of the audit. · HEMISPHERX will prepare a written report of the results of the audit and forward a copy to SCIEN. SCIEN will provide a written response to HEMISPHERX's written audit report within twenty (20) business days of receipt of such report setting forth the corrective actions to be taken by SCIEN, if any, and a timeline for such implementation. In the event of an inspection by any governmental or regulatory authority concerning the activities carried out under the Agreement, SCIEN shall notify HEMISPHERX promptly upon learning of such an inspection, shall supply HEMISPHERX with copies of any correspondence or portions of correspondence relating to HEMISPHERX's materials and shall inform HEMISPHERX of the general findings and outcomes of such inspections. SCIEN and HEMISPHERX shall cooperate with each other during any such inspection, investigation or other inquiry, including applying reasonable effort, as might be practical, at allowing, upon reasonable request, a representative of HEMISPHERX to be on site during such inspection, investigation or other inquiry, and providing copies of all documents related to the inspection. Each party acknowledges that it may not direct the manner in which the other party fulfills its obligations to permit inspection by governmental entities
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'reasonable advance notice during regular business hours at HEMISPHERX's principal office' creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifies a 20-day advance notice for audits. By removing the requirement for 'reasonable advance notice' and the location of the audit, the changed text conflicts with and undermines the specificity of the audit process outlined in the contradicted text, causing a strong in-text contradiction as the requirements for the audits are now ambigious and open to interpretation.
